heart.dataset_description: UCI Heart Disease dataset (Cleveland). Classification: predicts presence of heart disease (binary). Small dataset (303 rows, 14 columns). All features are already numeric — some (cp, thal, restecg) are integer-encoded categoricals that may benefit from one-hot encoding for linear/distance models. Very low missing rate (0.1%).

column_semantics.cp: nominal — chest pain type (0-3), integer-encoded categorical
column_semantics.ca: ordinal — number of major vessels colored by fluoroscopy (0-3)

must_do: encode_categorical_per_column: ordinal for ordered columns, one_hot for nominal
heart.notes: Linear/distance-based model (logreg): use one_hot for nominal categoricals to avoid false ordinal assumptions. Use ordinal ONLY for truly ordered columns. MUST clip outliers — linear and distance models are very sensitive to extreme values. Feature selection helps reduce multicollinearity.
